original_abstract:
Big Data Analytics and Deep Learning represent two of the most pivotal frontiers in modern data science. As both public and private organizations accumulate vast, domain-specific datasets, the ability to extract actionable insights has become essential for addressing critical challenges in fields such as national security, fraud detection, medical informatics, and strategic marketing. Industry leaders like Google and Microsoft increasingly rely on the analysis of massive data volumes to drive business intelligence and shape the trajectory of future technologies.

Deep Learning offers a powerful framework for this task, utilizing hierarchical learning processes to transform raw input into high-level, complex abstractions. By building sophisticated representations from simpler underlying features, these algorithms excel at processing the vast quantities of unsupervised, unlabeled data characteristic of the Big Data era. This paper explores the utility of Deep Learning in tackling core analytical problems, including pattern recognition in massive datasets, semantic indexing, automated data tagging, and accelerated information retrieval. Furthermore, we examine the specialized adaptations required to meet the demands of Big Data, such as managing high-dimensional streaming data, ensuring model scalability, and leveraging distributed computing architectures. We conclude by identifying key directions for future research, posing critical questions regarding data sampling criteria, domain adaptation, semi-supervised learning strategies, and the optimization of data abstractions.
humanized_abstract:
Big Data Analytics and Deep Learning are two of the most exciting areas of research in data science. As governmental and private organizations have an abundance of domain specific data, it has become necessary for organizations to have knowledge and skills to derive meaningful results from such data to solve complex problems such as in national security, anti-fraud, medical informatics and marketing. Companies such as Google and Microsoft lead the way by incorporating big data analytics into their business intelligence and are at the forefront of technologies such as AI and Deep learning.

Deep Learning: Understanding Big Data The emerging Deep Learning paradigm is a type of machine learning algorithm inspired by the human brain referred to as a network architecture with multiple layers. Such architecture enables transformation of the input data from low-level and simple features to high-level and complex features by iteratively applying learning to increase the abstraction level of the data. The Deep Learning algorithms are very beneficial when dealing with Big Data that contains large amount of unlabelled data. Here, the Deep Learning-based algorithms will be demonstrated with their applications in Big Data Analytics for identifying key use cases such as identification of patterns from large amounts of data, semantic search, data annotation, and acceleration of search operations. Challenges that arise when dealing with Big Data will also be discussed. For instance, dealing with high-dimensional stream data, model scalability, and distributed systems. Finally, some of the research areas that need investigation, and many open challenges and questions are identified, such as optimal data sampling approaches, domain adaptation for effective Big Data Analytics, suitable learning approaches for Big Data, and efficient data abstraction techniques.
